topic.name: Higher Education Act Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Higher Education Act of 1965, focusing on student loans, grants, and educational assistance programs.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to make the 3.4% interest rate on Direct Stafford loans...
applicable to Direct Stafford loans first disbursed to undergraduate students on or after July 1, 2012.
Document 2: Teacher Education Assistance Creating Hope for Our Future Act of 2011...
Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to expand the loan forgiveness available to public elementary and secondary school teachers.
Document 4: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to increase the maximum Pell Grant award...
Requires a further increase in any such maximum Pell Grant award, by an amount sufficient to achieve a 5% increase in students' purchasing power.
Document 5: Bank on Students Loan Fairness Act - Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965...
Sets the interest rate on Direct Stafford Loans first disbursed on or after July 1, 2013.
Document 9: Military and Veterans Education Protection Act...

amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require proprietary institutions of higher education to derive at least 10% of their revenue from sources other than federal educational assistance.

topic.name: Voting and Labor Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing voting rights, absentee voting for military personnel, and regulations surrounding labor representation and collective bargaining.

Document 1: Tribal Labor Sovereignty Act of 2013 - Amends the National Labor Relations Act to provide that any enterprise or institution owned and operated by an Indian tribe is not considered an employer.
Document 2: Amends the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act to permit a valid voter registration and absentee ballot request from overseas voters.
Document 3: Workforce Democracy and Fairness Act - Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the NLRB to direct an election by secret ballot as soon as practicable.
Document 4: Helping Heroes Vote Act of 2013 - Directs the Secretary of Defense to notify election officials of changes in servicemembers' mailing addresses.
Document 5: Vote Integrity and Verification Act of 2007 - Amends the Help America Vote Act to enhance ballot verification and accessibility for individuals with disabilities.
Document 6: Justice for Servicemembers Act of 2016 - Amends the Uniformed Services Employment Rights Act to protect employment rights of servicemembers.
Document 7: Vote Tabulation Audit Act of 2010 - Requires states to meet standards for transparent and accurate tabulation of votes.
Document 8: Voter Registration Modernization Act of 2009 - Directs states to ensure online voter registration availability.
Document 9: Leadership PAC Limitation Act - Prohibits the conversion of leadership PAC funds to personal use.
Document 10: Secret Ballot Protection Act - Makes it an unfair labor practice for employers to recognize a labor organization not selected by majority in a secret ballot.

topic.name: Alternative Fuels and Energy Efficiency
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting alternative fuels, energy efficiency, and reducing oil consumption through various programs and incentives.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an investment credit to eligible manufacturers for an advanced technology motor vehicle.
Directs the Secretary of Transportation to carry out evaluation projects to assess mileage-based road user charges.
Document 2: Establishes a national energy security program to reduce oil consumption by 8 million barrels per day by calendar 2030.
Directs the Secretary of Transportation and EPA to establish corporate average fuel economy standards.
Document 3: Establishes a federal energy efficiency resource standard for retail electricity and natural gas distributors.
Requires the Secretary of Energy to establish a program to implement, enforce, review, and adjust performance standards for specified cumulative electricity and natural gas savings.
Document 4: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to include projects for infrastructure for provision and distribution of alternative fuels.
Directs the Secretary to develop a methodology for calculating the equivalent volumes of conventional fuel displaced by use of each alternative fuel.
Document 5: Establishes a program to accelerate the commercial availability of carbon dioxide capture and storage technologies.
Requires the Corporation to collect an assessment on distribution utilities that shall reflect the relative carbon dioxide emission rates of different fossil fuel-based electricity.
Document 6: Amends the Petroleum Marketing Practices Act to revise the ethanol content requirements for renewable fuel.
Requires automobile manufacturers to ensure that at least 50% of 2015 and 2016 model year automobiles are dual fueled.
Document 7: Requires the Department of Transportation to designate Clean Vehicle Corridors and encourage cleaner alternative fuel options.
Defines 'cleaner alternative fuels' to include compressed natural gas, liquefied natural gas, and plug-in electric.
Document 8: Modifies the prohibition against assignment to a state of the responsibility of the Secretary of Transportation for conformity determination under the Clean Air Act.
Allows program participation by any state meeting the selection criteria.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Energy to establish a program to provide loan guarantees for the construction of facilities for the manufacture of advanced vehicle batteries.
Authorizes appropriations to encourage domestic production of plug-in electric hybrid vehicles.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving and regulating Medicare and Medicaid programs in the United States.

Document 1: establish an annual limit on out-of-pocket expenditures for Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries
eliminate the two-year waiting period for Medicare coverage with respect to individuals with disabilities
Document 2: expand the drug discount program to allow participation as a covered entity by certain children's hospitals
requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish reasonable exceptions to such prohibition
Document 3: continue entitlement to prescription drugs used in immunosuppressive therapy furnished to an individual who receives a kidney transplant
extend Medicare secondary payer requirements for end stage renal disease beneficiaries
Document 4: end with 2013 the current formula for an update to the single conversion factor in the formula for payment for physicians' services
establish a value-based performance incentive program
Document 5: revises and extends through FY2019 the program under title XXI State Children's Health Insurance
establishes in the Treasury the CHIP Shortfall Fund
Document 6: implement an asset verification program requiring Medicaid applicants to authorize the state to obtain records from a financial institution
requires states to submit to the Secretary state plan amendments for creating Internet-based transparency programs
Document 7: requires prompt payment of clean claims to pharmacies by prescription drug plans
defines prompt payment as payment occurring within 14 days from submission for claims submitted electronically
Document 8: directs the Department of Health and Human Services to select one Medicare administrative contractor to conduct a one-year demonstration program
HHS shall implement a process for the medical review of hospice care
Document 9: prohibits a group health plan from entering into a contract with any pharmacy benefit manager unless the PBM satisfies certain requirements
requires the PBM to report at least annually to each plan
Document 10: requires hospitals to implement staffing plans that meet specified ratios for direct care registered nurse-to-patient staffing levels
provides nurses with the right to refuse to accept assignments that would violate staffing requirements

topic.name: Criminal Justice Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming various aspects of criminal law, including drug offenses, gang activity, child exploitation, and firearms regulations.

Document 1: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to increase the amount of a controlled substance required for mandatory minimum prison terms for crack cocaine trafficking.
Directs the U.S. Sentencing Commission to review and amend its sentencing guidelines for trafficking in a controlled substance.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit certain criminal street gang crimes, including recruitment of persons into such gangs.
Impose or increase criminal penalties for committing certain violent crimes in aid of a criminal street gang.
Document 4: Impose a mandatory minimum prison term of two years for possession of child pornography and increase to 15 years the maximum prison term for such crime.
Make it a crime to knowingly access with intent to view child pornography.
Document 5: Authorize and direct the Attorney General and the Secretary of Homeland Security to expand resources to protect U.S. citizens and property.
Direct the Attorney General to dedicate and expand resources for the Project Gunrunner initiative to identify, investigate, and prosecute firearms trafficking.

topic.name: International Sanctions and Policies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislation regarding international sanctions, particularly related to countries such as Syria, North Korea, Iran, and Russia, addressing issues like human rights violations, nuclear proliferation, and territorial disputes.

Document 1: Directs the President to submit to Congress every 60 days a list of persons who are senior officials of the government of Syria.
Applies specified penalties under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to a U.S. person that conducts investment activities in Syria.
Document 2: Amends the Atomic Energy Act of 1954 to include as requirements to be met by a cooperating party to an agreement for peaceful nuclear cooperation.
Requires a joint congressional resolution of approval to a subsequent arrangement to a section 123 agreement.
Document 3: The President shall investigate any credible information of sanctionable activities by a person involving North Korea.

Requires the President to designate any person that knowingly engages in significant activities or transactions with North Korea that have materially contributed to the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction.
Document 4: States that it is U.S. policy to not recognize the de jure or de facto sovereignty of the Russian Federation over Crimea.
Prohibits any federal department or agency from taking any action that recognizes or implies recognition of sovereignty of the Russian Federation over Crimea.
Document 5: States that is U.S. policy to condemn the systemic violations of human rights conducted by the Syrian authorities.

Directs the President to prohibit the opening or maintaining in the United States of a correspondent or payable-through account by a foreign financial institution that has knowingly conducted any financial transaction with the Central Bank of Syria.
Document 6: Repeals or amends current laws restricting trade with Cuba.
Eliminates the prohibition on assistance to Cuba, and the President's authority for the embargo on Cuba.
Document 7: Applies to transfers of funds to or from Iran, or for the direct or indirect benefit of an Iranian person or the government of Iran.
The President may not, except for certain humanitarian purposes, issue any license under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.
Document 8: Amends the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996 to provide for the imposition of three or more sanctions with respect to a person who knowingly enters into an agreement relating to the purchase of petroleum resources from Iran.
Imposes sanctions on affiliates of Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC).
Document 9: States that it is the policy of the United States to support the decision of state and local governments and educational institutions to divest from, and to prohibit the investment of assets they control in, persons that have investments of more than $20 million in Iran's energy sector.
Expresses the sense of Congress that a state or local government should not adopt such measures against such a person unless it has made every effort to avoid erroneously targeting the person.
Document 10: Directs the President, upon receipt of credible information about sanctionable activities involving the government of North Korea, to investigate the possible designation for sanctions.
Requires the President to brief appropriate congressional committees, within 180 days after enactment of this Act, on efforts to implement it.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on various chemical compounds and mixtures.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on mixtures of benzenesulfonic acid...
Document discusses specific chemical mixtures and their tariff duties.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on acetic acid...
Document focuses on the temporary suspension of duty on a specific chemical.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on aqueous emulsion...

Document outlines amendments related to specific chemical mixtures.
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the suspension of duty on macroporous ion-exchange resin...
Document addresses tariff changes for a type of resin.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to revise the heading of a specified chemical...
Document specifies changes to tariff classifications for certain chemicals.
Document 8: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on Acetic acid...

topic.name: Transportation Security and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation focused on enhancing security measures and regulatory frameworks within various transportation sectors in the United States.

Document 1: Declares that a valid Transportation Worker Identification Credential (TWIC) issued before enactment of this Act... shall not expire before...
the Secretary's authority to revoke a TWIC because: (1) a TWIC holder is not qualified to hold it; or (2) the credential is lost, damaged, or stolen.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT)... to modify a specified Department of Transportation (DOT) final rule relating to flightcrew member duty and rest requirements...
the requirements under that rule apply to all-cargo flightcrew members to the same extent they apply to air passenger flightcrew members.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS)... to establish at each U.S. airport federal air cargo screening centers to screen cargo transported on domestic and foreign passenger aircraft...
establish minimum standards for equipment, technology, procedures, personnel, and methods used to conduct such screening.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to issue regulations prohibiting an individual on an aircraft from engaging in voice communications using a mobile device during a flight...
Exempts from such prohibition: (1) on-duty flight crews and attendants, and (2) federal law enforcement officers acting in an official capacity.
Document 9: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to issue an order to require: (1) the installation of secondary barriers to prevent access to the flight deck of a commercial aircraft;
with respect to an aircraft that is equipped with a cockpit door, that such barriers remain locked while the aircraft is in flight.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Conservation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. agricultural legislation aimed at improving food security, conservation practices, and risk management for farmers.

Document 1: Amends the Food Security Act of 1985 to extend funding for the environmental quality incentives program.
Provides a cost-share payment exception for limited resource and beginning farmers.
Document 2: Repeals direct payments under the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008.
Continues countercyclical payments and ACRE for crop year 2013.
Document 3: Defines specified terms related to commodity programs.
Requires election of either direct payments and counter-cyclical payments or conservation payments.
Document 4: States that it is U.S. policy to promote global food security, improve agricultural productivity.
Expands the scope of agricultural research to include biotechnological advances.
Document 5: Establishes the agriculture risk coverage program through crop year 2018 for covered crops.
Requires producers to choose individual or county coverage.
Document 6: Prohibits any direct, counter-cyclical or transition payment to an individual or entity whose adjusted gross income exceeds $200,000.
Links such payments to environmental stewardship.
Document 7: Repeals, but continues for crop year 2013, direct payments, countercyclical payments, and the average crop revenue election program.
Establishes a dairy production margin protection program.
Document 8: Reauthorizes the Clean Water Act State Revolving Fund and the State Drinking Water Act SRF.
Directs the Administrator to assist states in establishing simplified procedures for small systems.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to make state grants for extension, education, applied research.
Establishes a federal-state matching grant program.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Authorizes funds for monitoring and assessment, research, pollution reduction.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax credits and deductions for different groups and situations.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow Indian tribes to make the determination that a child is a child with special needs for purposes of the adoption tax credit.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income distributions from an individual retirement account and other tax-exempt retirement plans used to pay long-term care insurance premiums.
Document 3: Imposes a federal income tax rate of 15% on all income of all U.S. citizens, regardless of annual income or current economic standing.
Document 4: Makes permanent provisions of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 and the Working Families Tax Relief Act of 2004 that eliminate the marriage penalty in the standard deduction.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income student loan repayments by the federal government on behalf of federal employees.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow certain small business employers a work opportunity tax credit for hiring a member of the Ready Reserve or the National Guard.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow certain small employers and their employees tax credits for health insurance costs.
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income distributions from individual retirement accounts for certain charitable purposes.
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from the taxable income of a life insurance company any non-taxed reinsurance premium.
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow certain employers a business-related tax credit for wages paid during family and medical leave.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, focusing on the suspension and modification of duties on specific imported goods.

Document 1: this bill amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend for 2016 through 2025 duty-free treatment of certain textile goods imported into the United States from Morocco.
Annual quantitative limitations are prescribed for the preferential tariff treatment of such goods imported into the United States during that period.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to: (1) modify the article description of certain low expansion stoppers, lids, and other closures; and (2) extend the temporary suspension of duty on such articles.
extend the temporary suspension of duty on disperse yellow 64.

to reduce temporarily the duty on parts of machinery for the industrial preparation or manufacture of dried vegetable snack items.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to reduce temporarily the duty on parts of machinery for the industrial preparation or manufacture of dried vegetable snack items.

Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to: (1) modify the article description of metal halide lamps designed for use in video projectors, and (2) extend temporarily the suspension of duty on such articles.
Document 5: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain women's footwear covering the ankle.
suspend temporarily the duty on certain women's footwear with outer soles and uppers of rubber or plastics and valued over $12 but not over $20 per pair.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain mechanics' work gloves.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain staple fibers of viscose rayon.

topic.name: Maritime and Aquatic Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. legislation related to maritime activities, aquatic resources, and environmental protection measures in waterways.

Document 1: Amends the Water Resources Development Act of 2007 to include projects for navigation at Northeast Harbor and Tenants Harbor, Maine.
projects that are deauthorized by such Act.
Document 2: Maritime Lien Reform Act
prohibit maritime liens from being imposed on state or federal fishing permits.
Document 3: Extends provisions prohibiting the EPA from requiring an NPDES permit for covered vessels.
for any discharge that is incidental to the normal operation of a covered vessel.
Document 4: Defending Our Great Lakes Act of 2015
to prevent the upstream transfer of aquatic nuisance species from the Mississippi River basin to the Great Lakes basin.
Document 6: Red Snapper Management Improvement Act
exclusive authority to manage and conserve the red snapper in both the waters of each Gulf coastal state.
Document 7: Amends federal shipping law to extend through October 31, 2028, the exemption of certain vessels from fire-retardant materials requirement.
requires the owner or managing operator of an exempt vessel to notify crew members.
Document 8: Great Lakes Asian Carp Barrier Act
to prevent the spread of aquatic nuisance species between the Great Lakes and Mississippi River basins.
Document 10: Extends the time period during which the EPA is prohibited from requiring a National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System permit.
for any discharge that is incidental to the normal operation of those vessels.

topic.name: Unemployment and Social Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at reforming unemployment benefits and social security programs in the United States.

Document 1: Pathways to Independence Act of 2007 - Amends part A (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families) (TANF) of title IV of the Social Security Act
deem individuals with disabilities complying with a modified employability plan to be meeting work participation requirements.
Document 2: Supplemental Security Income Restoration Act of 2015

Amends title XVI (Supplemental Security Income) (SSI) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to increase from $240 to $1,344 the first amount of general income per year excluded in determining SSI program eligibility.
Document 3: Emergency Unemployment Benefits Extension Act of 2010 - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA)
Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program through January 3, 2012.
Document 4: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2014 - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008
to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before July 1, 2014.
Document 5: Unemployment Assistance Act of 2009 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income amounts distributed from tax-exempt retirement plans
to pay for certain living, health care and education or job training expenses of a taxpayer during a period of unemployment.
Document 6: Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation Pilots Equitable Treatment Act - Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA)
to require the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation (PBGC) to compute the actuarial value of monthly benefits for commercial airline pilots.
Document 7: Extended Unemployment Benefits Reform Act of 2012 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to prohibit state payment of extended UC to an individual
for any week in which he or she does not perform at least 20 hours of unpaid public service.
Document 8: Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2008 - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to revise the formula for Tier-1 amounts
increases the figures in the formula from: 50% to 80% of the total amount of regular compensation.
Document 9: Generating Real Opportunities for Workers and Transitional Help Act or GROWTH Act - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008
to declare that nothing in the Act shall prevent the commencement or continued payment of first-tier emergency unemployment compensation.
Document 10: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments
for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before April 1, 2014.

topic.name: Tax Credits and Deductions
topic.description: Legislation focused on various tax credits and deductions aimed at supporting families, education, caregiving, and specific industries.
