Document 7: include meals away from home within the deduction.
Document 8: eliminate the mandatory reduction in the rate of the tax credit for household and dependent care services.
Document 9: allow individual taxpayers a tax deduction for the qualified expenses of attending a private school.
Document 10: extend through 2015 the tax credit for differential wage payments to employees who are active duty members of the uniformed services.

topic.name: Federal Recognition and Land Trust for Tribes
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures related to the federal recognition of various Indian tribes and the management of land trust for their benefit.

Document 1: Amends the Omnibus Indian Advancement Act to repeal the declaration that specified land accepted by the Secretary of the Interior for the benefit of the Lytton Rancheria in California shall be deemed to have been held in trust.
Allows the Lytton Rancheria to conduct class II gaming activities on the land taken into trust.
Document 2: Provides for the consideration of a petition for federal recognition as an Indian tribe by the Lumbee Indians of Robeson and adjoining counties of North Carolina.
Expresses the sense of the Congress that review of such petition should not unnecessarily delay review of petitions awaiting active consideration.
Document 4: Extends federal recognition to the Lumbee Tribe of North Carolina.
Makes the Tribe and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided to Indians because of their status as members of a federally recognized tribe.
Document 6: This bill amends the Siletz Tribe Indian Restoration Act to authorize the Department of the Interior to take into trust for the Siletz Tribe additional lands that lie within the original 1855 Siletz Coast Reservation.
Such land must be considered and evaluated as an on-reservation acquisition and become part of the tribe's reservation.
Document 8: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana.
Directs the Tribe to acquire, for the benefit of the Tribe, trust title to 200 acres of land within the Tribe's service area to be used for a tribal land base.
Document 9: Extends federal recognition to the following Indian tribes of Virginia and establishes their relationship with the federal government.
Makes the tribes and their members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized Indian tribes.

topic.name: Legislative Actions and Appropriations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to appropriations, boundary modifications, performance assessments, and memorials.

Document 1: Amends the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 1987 to allow the Clerk of the House of Representatives to donate computers and related peripheral tools to a public elementary or secondary school.
without regard to whether the donation is a cost to the government; or has a recoverable value.
Document 2: Modifies the boundary of Minute Man National Historical Park in Massachusetts to include the Colonel James Barrett Farm in Concord.
Document 3: Directs the Comptroller General to submit to Congress an assessment of the performance of the major automated information system programs of the Department of Defense.
Requires a preliminary report by the end of FY2012.
Document 4: Treats the measure of unemployment issued by the Bureau of Labor Statistics (known as U5) as the official and primary measure of unemployment in the United States.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of Defense to provide funds to states submitting specified plans for the National Guard to supplement state and local efforts to keep schools safe from violence.
Document 6: Amends the Office of National Drug Control Policy Reauthorization Act of 2006 to authorize appropriations for the U.S. Anti-Doping Agency for FY2014-FY2020.
Require that the Agency be recognized worldwide as the independent national anti-doping organization for the United States.
Document 7: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to place in Arlington National Cemetery an American Braille tactile flag honoring blind members of the Armed Forces.
Grants the Secretary exclusive authority to approve an appropriate design and site within Arlington National Cemetery for the memorial.
Document 8: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to require that one of the presidential appointees to the Board of Directors of the FDIC once served as a state bank supervisor.
Document 9: Includes National Vietnam War Veterans Day, March 29, among those days on which the flag should especially be displayed.
Document 10: Amends the Inspector General Act of 1978 and the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to create an Inspector General for the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau.
Requires the President, within 60 days after enactment of this Act, to appoint a CFPB Inspector General.

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing consumer protection in financial transactions and credit services.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to requirements for disclosure to a consumer of points and fees information
Excludes from points and fees any such reasonable charges even though a creditor receives compensation
Document 2: Requires the annual report on the implementation of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act to be made available to the public
Amends the Act to presume that in any civil action, claim, or related appeal brought under the Act the deficiency amounts reported to Congress accurately reflect the minimum damages
Document 3: Prohibits a deferred presentment transaction except as provided under this Act
Requires a transaction agreement to limit the interest and fees to no more than 10% of the transaction amount
Document 4: Prescribes consumer protection guidelines for rental-purchase transactions
Grants enforcement powers to the Federal Trade Commission and to state attorneys general
Document 5: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act to authorize the issuance of remotely created checks only if the consumer has designated in writing a person to issue them
Empowers the CFPB to investigate violations of this Act
Document 6: Prohibits debt settlement providers from providing a debt settlement service or receiving a fee from a consumer without a signed written contract
Requires a debt settlement provider who receives funds from a consumer to hold them for a consumer settlement account
Document 7: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require advance notice of any increase in the annual percentage rate of interest
Prohibits its imposition in a subsequent billing cycle with respect to such excess credit
Document 9: Prohibits a depository institution from engaging in unfair or deceptive acts or practices in connection with overdraft coverage
Requires each depository institution that provides overdraft coverage for transaction accounts clearly and conspicuously to disclose overdraft coverage fees

topic.name: Campaign Finance and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to campaign finance reform, transparency in political contributions, and regulations governing lobbying and communication practices in the electoral process.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to repeal the limitation on party expenditures on behalf of candidates in general elections.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require all election-related designations, statements, and reports required to be filed under the Act to be filed directly with the Federal Election Commission (FEC).
Document 7: Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to require the application for registration of a security on a national securities exchange... to contain a statement certifying that the issuer is in compliance with the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971.

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Infrastructure and Technology
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving infrastructure, technology, and communication services in the United States.

Document 1: Protect Our Devices Act of 2016 prohibits federal government from requiring assistance in breaking encryption.
Document 2: Broadband Opportunity and Affordability Act requires FCC to expand Lifeline program for low-income consumers.
Document 3: Increasing America's Global Development Capacity Act requires increase in full-time Foreign Service officers by USAID.
Document 4: Freedom From Foreign Influence Act directs permits for energy resources exploration in the Arctic.
Document 5: Agent Orange Equity Act includes territorial seas for veterans' service connection.
Document 6: Keystone For a Secure Tomorrow Act approves permits for Keystone XL pipeline project.
Document 7: Rail Crossings Safety Improvement Act reauthorizes appropriations for rail line improvement projects.
Document 8: Driver Fatigue Prevention Act amends Fair Labor Standards Act for over-the-road bus drivers.
Document 9: Freight Rail Economic Development Act directs grants for freight rail economic development projects.
Document 10: State Fisheries Act amends Magnuson-Stevens Act for fisheries management in the Gulf of Mexico.

topic.name: Intelligence and National Security Funding
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the authorization and funding of intelligence and national security activities in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2012 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2012, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 2: Directs the federal government to conduct cybersecurity activities to provide shared situational awareness
Defines 'shared situational awareness' as an environment where cyber threat information is shared in real time.
Document 3: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to extend through FY2017 the authorization of appropriations for the bulletproof vest partnership grant program
Expresses the sense of Congress that amounts made available to carry out the Bulletproof Vest Partnership Grant Program should be made available through the end of the fiscal year.
Document 4: Authorizes the Attorney General to maintain public databases containing missing persons records
Conduct a study on how to better integrate the national missing persons databases.
Document 5: Expresses the sense of Congress that the President should pursue bilateral and multilateral international agreements
Develop expedited protocols for the data exchange and dissemination of sensitive information needed to publicly identify the source of a nuclear detonation.
Document 6: States as the purpose of this Act the protection of children from sexual exploitation
Requires sex offenders who are U.S. citizens or permanent residents to notify a jurisdiction of their intent to travel.
Document 7: Authorizes FY2016 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Allows the DNI, if it provides prior notification to Congress, to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized for FY2016.
Document 8: Authorizes appropriations for FY2007 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2007, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 9: Authorizes the Attorney General to make grants to provide improved security at institutions of higher education
Establish an interagency task force to develop and promulgate a set of advisory school safety guidelines.
Document 10: Authorizes appropriations for FY2013 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Allows the DNI to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized for FY2013.

topic.name: Immigration and Employment Policies
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to immigration, employment, and workforce regulations in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the wages or other remuneration earned by an employee who performs employment duties in more than one state from being subject to income tax in any state other than the state of the employee's residence.
Exempts employers from withholding of tax and information reporting requirements for employees not subject to income tax under this Act.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to confer 'blue card status' upon an alien who has performed agricultural employment in the United States for at least 863 hours or 150 work days.
Adjusts a blue card alien (and spouse and minor children) to permanent resident status if the alien has fulfilled specified periods of agricultural employment.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Technical Corrections Act of 1994 to make the J-1 visa waiver program permanent.
Revises entry provisions for H-1B visa foreign medical graduates and provide for an increase under specified conditions in the annual per-state cap of 30 foreign doctors.
Document 4: Eliminates the per country numerical limitation for employment-based immigrants.
Sets forth the following transition period for employment-based second and third preference (EB-2 and EB-3) immigrant visas.
Document 5: Revises employer and government requirements with respect to H-1B and L-1 nonimmigrant aliens.
Provides H-1B and L-1 alien whistleblower protections.
Document 6: Makes up to 10% of the worldwide employment-based immigration level available to qualified immigrants who hold a doctorate degree in a STEM field.
The permanent priority date for any employment-based petition shall be the date on which the petition is filed with Department of Homeland Security.
Document 7: Authorizes an employee to request from an employer a change in the terms or conditions of the employee's employment.
Makes it unlawful for an employer to interfere with any rights provided to an employee under this Act.
Document 8: Entitles an eligible employee to up to 12 workweeks of leave during any 12-month period to attend court proceedings relating to the prosecution of a person for a violent crime.
Document 9: Directs the Department of Homeland Security to authorize employment for an asylum applicant who is not in detention and whose asylum application has not been determined to be frivolous.
Employment authorization shall be for a period of one year, and renewable for additional one-year periods.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of State to facilitate the expedited review of J-visa applications of aliens applying for U.S. admission to participate in a graduate medical education or training program.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at ensuring transparency, accountability, and proper governance in various federal and state operations.

Document 1: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment of Representatives
Requires such redistricting to be conducted through a plan developed by the independent redistricting commission established in the state
Document 2: Confers exclusive jurisdiction upon the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia to hear causes and claims arising from a covered energy project
Prescribes a 180-day deadline for court resolution of a complaint or cause of action
Document 3: This bill reduces discretionary spending by 0.5% for federal agencies that have either not submitted a financial statement
The bill establishes reporting requirements for the Office of Management and Budget and DOD
Document 4: Authorizes the National Capital Planning Commission to modify the boundaries of Areas I and II of the National Mall
Directs the Commission to prepare recommendations for improving upon the process required for the selection, design, and subject matter of commemorative works
Document 5: Amends the District of Columbia Official Code to require the chief judge of the District of Columbia Court of Appeals to call biennial or annual judicial conferences
Authorizes the chief judges of the District Superior Court and of the District Court of Appeals to toll or delay judicial proceedings in certain natural disaster or other emergency situations
Document 6: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment of Representatives
Requires such redistricting to be conducted according to a plan developed by the independent redistricting commission established in the state
Document 7: Requires the Majority and Minority Leaders of the Senate to appoint the first IG
Requires the IG to audit and investigate Senate programs and operations
Document 8: Prohibits a final agency rule from being published or taking effect until the agency submits the rule to the Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs
Defines a 'high-impact rule' as any rule that the OIRA determines may impose an annual cost on the economy of at least $1 billion
Document 9: Prohibits the government from approving an amendment to the Washington Metropolitan Area Transit Authority Compact unless its signatories certify qualifications for Board of Directors
The Department of Transportation shall ensure that the next three federal appointments to the Board have such qualifications
Document 10: Revises requirements for printing the Congressional Record, specifying that it be made electronically available promptly
Prohibits the Government Printing Office from printing more than the minimum number of copies of the Record necessary for archival purposes

topic.name: Clean Energy Tax Incentives
topic.description: The documents discuss various tax incentives and credits aimed at promoting clean and renewable energy sources, including wind, solar, and biomass.

Document 1: extends various energy production and conservation provisions
tax credits for producing electricity from certain renewable resources
Document 2: allow a 30% tax credit for investment in a qualifying offshore wind facility
direct the Department of the Treasury to establish a qualifying credit for offshore wind facilities program
Document 4: allow a tax credit for pollution abatement equipment
extend through 2013 the tax credit for producing electricity from renewable resources
Document 5: extend through 2019 the tax credit for producing electricity from renewable wind resources
allow such credit as an offset against the alternative minimum tax
Document 10: allow tax credits through 2019 for investment in an offshore facility using wind to produce electricity
defines 'offshore facility' to mean any facility located in the inland navigable waters of the United States

topic.name: Small Business Procurement Programs
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing procurement opportunities and support for various categories of small businesses, including those owned by women, veterans, and socially disadvantaged groups.

Document 1: Women's Procurement Program Improvement Act of 2012 - Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the procurement program for women-owned small businesses
providing a federal procurement contracting preference to such businesses
Document 2: Service Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business Procurement Reform Act of 2009 - Amends the Small Business Act to subject to certain penalties any person misrepresenting the status of a business
Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish an Office of Service Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business Procurement
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act to exclude from the definition of 'Indian tribe' any Alaska Native Corporation (ANC) or Alaska Native Village (ANV)
Makes members of socially and economically disadvantaged ANCs or ANVs eligible for such loans
Document 4: Securing Equity for the Economic Development of Low Income Areas Act of 2007 or SEED Act - Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958

Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to select at least one company from each SBA geographic region when selecting companies to participate as New Markets Venture Capital companies
Document 5: Small Business Access to Capital Act of 2014 - Amends the Small Business Jobs Act of 2010 to extend for an additional eight fiscal years the State Small Business Credit Initiative
Prescribes allocations of federal funds to participating states
Document 6: Veterans Small Business Enhancement Act of 2015 - Amends the Small Business Act to require the interagency task force that is responsible for coordinating federal efforts to improve the business development
to small business concerns owned and controlled by veterans
Document 7: Creating Jobs From Innovative Small Businesses Act of 2011 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a general business tax credit of 20% of the amount paid to acquire an equity investment in a qualified high technology small business concern
Document 8: Enhanced New Markets and Expanded Investment in Renewable Energy for Small Manufacturers Act of 2009 - Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to require the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to participate in the new markets venture capital program
Document 9: Improving Small Business Advocacy Act of 2016 - This bill amends the Small Business Act to declare that the exclusive duties of a Business Opportunity Specialist
shall be to implement specified SBA loan programs
Document 10: Veterans Small Business Opportunity Act of 2007 - Amends the Small Business Act to extend certain small business loan time limits with respect to a small business owned and controlled by a veteran
Directs the Secretary of Defense to conduct a study for promoting positive working relations between employers and their reserve component employees

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Regulations
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at protecting personal information and ensuring data security across various sectors.

Document 1: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations requiring any person engaged in interstate commerce that owns or possesses data containing personal information to establish and implement reasonable security policies and procedures.
Establishes notification procedures in the event of a breach of security of any system that contains personal information.
Document 2: Prohibits a federal agency from deploying or making available to the public a new federal personally identifiable information website until the chief information officer of the agency submits a certification to Congress that the website is fully functional and secure.

Directs the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to establish and oversee policies and procedures for federal agencies to follow in the event of a breach of information security involving the disclosure of personally identifiable information.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to authorize a governmental entity to require the disclosure by a provider of electronic communication service or remote computing service of the contents of a wire or electronic communication that is in electronic storage with or otherwise stored, held, or maintained by the provider only pursuant to a warrant issued by a court of competent jurisdiction.
Sets forth requirements for government notification of provider customers or subscribers regarding the receipt of communication contents pursuant to such a warrant.
Document 4: Requires a commercial provider of an electronic communication service to retain for at least one year a log of the temporarily assigned network addresses assigned to subscribers or customers that enables the identification of corresponding customer or subscriber information.
Directs the Attorney General to study and report within two years on the provider compliance costs.
Document 5: Requires certain commercial entities that acquire, maintain, store, or utilize individuals' nonpublic personal information to protect and secure any such data that is held unencrypted in electronic form.
Directs entities that own or license such data, following discovery of a security breach, to notify each individual U.S. citizen or resident.
Document 6: This bill amends the Electronic Communications Privacy Act of 1986 to make a series of technical changes to clarify that the voluntary disclosure prohibitions and exceptions to such prohibitions apply to the content of communications regardless of whether the communications are held in storage or have been read.

A governmental entity may apply for a court order directing a provider, for up to 180 days, to refrain from notifying any person that the provider has been required to disclose communications or records.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to study and identify any potential threats to privacy protections posed by the integration of unmanned aircraft (drone) systems into the national airspace system.
Prohibits a governmental entity from using a drone system, or requesting information or data collected, without adhering to privacy principles.
Document 8: Makes it unlawful to acquire or use customer proprietary network information without written consent.
Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to require each telecommunications carrier to notify customers of unlawful disclosure of such information.
Document 9: This bill requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations requiring entities regulated by the FTC, common carriers, and nonprofit organizations to establish information security practices for the treatment and protection of personal information.
Within 10 days following discovery of a security breach, entities must notify the FTC; the Federal Bureau of Investigation; the U.S. Secret Service.
Document 10: Amends the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act of 1998 to apply the prohibitions against collecting personal information from children to online applications and mobile applications directed to children.
Establishes additional privacy protections against the collection of personal or geolocation information from children and minors.

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Reform
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving the efficiency, accountability, and oversight of federal government operations and regulations.

Document 1: Establishes the Federal Realignment and Closure Commission, which shall: (1) establish a systematic method for assessing the effectiveness and accountability of agency programs
recommend the realignment or elimination of any agency or program that has wasted federal funds
Document 2: Requires the Office of Advocacy (Office) of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to carry out responsibilities concerning the analysis of regulatory functions
each federal agency to notify the SBA's Chief Counsel for Advocacy of any agency draft rules that may have a significant impact on a substantial number of small businesses
Document 3: require federal agencies to prepare and publish in the Federal Register an initial and final regulatory impact analysis prior to promulgating any proposed or final rule
make consideration of the least burdensome alternative to a rule mandatory
Document 4: Establishes the Grace Commission II to conduct a review of cost control in the federal government every two years
recommendations regarding opportunities for increased efficiency and reduced costs
Document 5: helping prime federal government contractors to find small business concerns capable of performing subcontracts
conducting periodic reviews of such contractors to assess compliance with required subcontracting plans
Document 6: directs the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to establish a Research Policy Board to review proposed federal regulations
OMB shall periodically review estimates of the hours spent by research institutions in meeting the burdens imposed by federal research funding agencies
Document 7: Requires the Director of the Congressional Budget Office (CBO) to establish and maintain a separate Regulatory Analysis Division
provide analysis during the public comment period of the prospective economic impact of economically significant proposed rules
Document 9: requires the Office of Management and Budget to coordinate with relevant federal agencies to eliminate, consolidate, or streamline federal programs and agencies with duplicate and overlapping missions
determine the total cost savings from the use of such authority

topic.name: Veterans Health Care and Support
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative measures aimed at improving health care access, mental health support, and overall well-being for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to use the Secretary's authority to employ certain personnel without regard to civil service or classification laws, rules, or regulations to employ physicians.
Conditions an uncompensated physician's receipt of a credential or privilege to practice medicine in a VA facility on the physician's commitment to serve at least 40 hours in the facility.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to determine whether or not to furnish such care or services to a veteran in a non-VA facility.
Requires such determination to be made within 30 days following the request for such care or services.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to provide financial assistance to eligible entities to provide and coordinate the provision of various supportive services for very low-income veteran families.
Requires equitable geographic distribution of such assistance.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to make interactive training sessions available for family caregivers.
Makes family caregivers eligible for counseling, training, and mental health services under existing provisions.
Document 5: Removes the requirement that family members of a veteran being treated in a Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) hospital for a non-service-connected disability to be eligible for counseling, training, and mental health services.
The continued provision of such services on an outpatient basis is essential to permit the veteran's discharge from the hospital.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to classify military working dogs as canine members of the Armed Forces.
Requires that such dogs no longer be classified as equipment.
Document 7: This bill declares that a veteran who is enrolled in the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) health care program requests a medical examination or treatment at an emergency department of a VA medical facility.
The VA is deemed to have met such requirements if it offers to transfer the veteran to another medical facility.
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Defense to enhance the suicide prevention program of the Department of Defense (DOD).
Requires the Secretary of each military department to include a training component on suicide prevention.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to prescribe standards for appointment and practice as a physician within the Veterans Health Administration (VHA).
Prohibits a new appointment without approval of the director of the Veterans Integrated Services Network.
Document 10: Expresses the sense of Congress that members of the Armed Forces deserve the best possible treatment for mental and physical illnesses.
There is a continued need for research, new treatments, and best practices for treating PTSD.

topic.name: Energy Research and Development
topic.description: Documents focus on various aspects of energy research, development, and policy aimed at improving energy efficiency, renewable energy technologies, and workforce training in the energy sector.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Energy for research, development, demonstration, and commercial application of vehicles and related technologies

conduct a program of basic and applied research, development, engineering, demonstration, and commercial application activities on materials, technologies, and processes with the potential to substantially reduce or eliminate petroleum use and related emissions
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Energy to expand the Energy Star program to include the certification of buildings as green buildings
Defines 'green building' as a building constructed taking into account sustainable site development, water savings, energy efficiency, materials selection
Document 3: Instructs the Secretary of Energy to support research and demonstration programs to expand marine renewable energy production
establishment of protocols, in conjunction with the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), for how the ocean community may best interact with marine renewable energy devices
Document 4: DOE is directed to prioritize education and training for energy and manufacturing jobs
establish a clearinghouse for information and guidance on job training and other workforce development programs for energy and manufacturing jobs
Document 5: Directs the President to establish a multiyear policy, partnership, and funding strategy to assist countries in sub-Saharan Africa develop an appropriate mix of power solutions
USAID should prioritize where loan guarantees to African financial institutions would facilitate involvement in African power projects
Document 6: Authorizes the head of each executive agency to establish projects under which an agency head may offer to enter into cost-effective power purchase agreements
establish a federal facility energy efficiency and renewable energy projects fund program
Document 7: This bill amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 by requiring the Department of Energy (DOE) to carry out the Solar Fuels Basic Research Initiative
support research about replicating natural photosynthetic processes in artificial photosynthesis processes
Document 8: Declares it is the policy of the United States to cooperate with India to address common energy challenges
collaborate with India in energy research that fosters market-based approaches to energy security
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of State to seek to establish the Western Hemisphere Energy Cooperation Forum to promote renewable energy production
Directs the Secretary and the Secretary of Energy to seek to establish an Oil and Gas Working Group to strengthen oil- and natural gas-related dialogue
Document 10: Directs the President to establish a Green Chemistry Research and Development Program to promote and coordinate federal research
Requires the President to establish an Interagency Working Group to oversee the planning, management, and coordination of the Program

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Conveyance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the management, sale, withdrawal, and environmental protection of federal lands across various states.

Document 1: provides for the sale of specified federal land in Winn Parish, Louisiana
requires sale proceeds to be used for the acquisition of lands and interests in the Kisatchie National Forest
Document 2: authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to accept the quitclaim, disclaimer, and relinquishment by the Manitou and Pikes Peak Railway Company
specified right-of-way adjacent to Pike National Forest
Document 3: directs the Department of Agriculture to permit by special use authorization nonmotorized access and use of the Bolts Ditch
for the diversion of water and use, maintenance, and repair of the ditch and headgate
Document 4: directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to the Henderson Redevelopment Agency
for the environmental remediation and reclamation of the Site
Document 5: withdraws specified Bureau of the Land Management (BLM) lands in Placitas, New Mexico
from all forms of mineral development under all laws pertaining to mineral leasing
Document 6: directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue a quitclaim deed conveying any right, title, and interest the United States may have
to the City of Albuquerque
Document 7: directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Park City, Utah, two parcels of land
requires the Secretary to conduct sales of the federal land identified as parcels 17 and 18
Document 8: amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act to make corrections to the segment designations for the Chetco River
withdraws the federal land within the boundaries of a certain segment of the Chetco River
Document 9: withdraws certain federal land located in the Sloan Hills area of Clark County, Nevada
from location, entry, and patent under the mining laws
Document 10: provides for the disposition of four parcels of land in California
directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey approximately 41.15 acres designated as Morongo lands

topic.name: Disaster Risk Reduction and Infrastructure Improvement
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing disaster preparedness, risk reduction, and the improvement of infrastructure to safeguard communities from natural hazards.

Document 1: Amends the National Windstorm Impact Reduction Act of 2004 to revise provisions governing the National Windstorm Impact Reduction Program.
Requires NIST to ensure that the Program includes the necessary components to promote implementation of windstorm risk reduction measures.
Document 2: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to provide competitive grants to establish and maintain centers of excellence for green infrastructure.
Defines 'green infrastructure' as a stormwater management technique that preserves, restores, enhances, or mimics natural hydrology.
Document 4: Requires the Under Secretary of Commerce for Oceans and Atmosphere to prioritize weather-related activities, including improved forecasts for the protection of life and property.
Directs the Assistant Administrator to conduct a program to develop an improved understanding of forecast capabilities for atmospheric events.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code addressing tax provisions for individuals and businesses.

Document 1: Equal Dignity for Married Taxpayers Act of 2015
amends the Internal Revenue Code to change provisions that refer to married couples
Document 2: Veterans Employment Today Act of 2010
amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the work opportunity tax credit for the hiring of disabled and unemployed veterans.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the tax deduction for state and local general sales taxes.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce from $18 to $9 the per-barrel tax on beer.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income government payments for the replacement of hazardous drywall.
Document 6: Tax Compromise Improvement Act of 2009
amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the requirement for partial payment of tax liabilities.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the limitation on distilled spirits excise taxes.
Document 8: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is Act of 2008
amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow taxpayers to designate payments on their tax returns.
Document 9: Border Fence Trust Fund Act of 2008
amends the Internal Revenue Code to establish the Border Improvement Trust Fund.
Document 10: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the expensing allowance for qualified film, television, and live theatrical productions.

topic.name: Defense Procurement and Authorization
topic.description: The documents focus on the authorization and appropriation of funds for military procurement and operations across various branches of the U.S. Armed Forces and the Coast Guard.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force for aircraft, missiles, weapons and tracked combat vehicles, ammunition, shipbuilding and conversion, the Joint Improvised Explosive Device Defeat Fund, and other procurement.
Prohibits the use of specified Army procurement funds to close the production line for the Army Tactical Missile System program.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2010 for the Army, Navy and Marine Corps, and Air Force for aircraft, missiles, weapons and tracked combat vehicles, ammunition, shipbuilding and conversion, and other procurement.
Prohibits the Secretary of Defense from procuring more than one Future Combat Systems spin out early-infantry brigade combat team equipment set for low-rate initial production.
Document 3: Authorizes FY2015 appropriations for the Coast Guard for operation and maintenance; the acquisition, construction, rebuilding, and improvement of aids to navigation, shore and offshore facilities, vessels, and aircraft.

Research, development, test, and evaluation of technologies, materials, and human factors related to search and rescue, aids to navigation, marine safety, marine environmental protection, enforcement of laws and treaties.

topic.name: Tax Relief Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at providing tax relief and modifications to the Internal Revenue Code.

Document 1: Charitable Tax Relief Act of 2007
exclusion from gross income of distributions from individual retirement accounts made for charitable purposes.
Document 2: Repeals provisions in the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent tax deductions for state and local sales taxes.
Document 3: Tax Relief Act of 2012
extends the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001.
Document 4: Senior Assets Verification Exemption (SAVE) from Capital Gains Tax Act of 2009
allows a tax deduction for the qualified net capital gain of taxpayers age 65 and older.
Document 5: Estate Tax Reduction Act of 2007
reduce to 39.2% the maximum estate tax rate.
Document 6: Comprehensive 1099 Taxpayer Protection and Repayment of Exchange Subsidy Overpayments Act of 2011
repeal requirements for the reporting to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS).
Document 7: Family Farm, Small Business, and Home Tax Relief Act
reduce the maximum estate tax rate from 49 to 43% in 2008.
Document 8: End Tax Uncertainty Act of 2011
Makes permanent the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001.
Document 9: Commercial Fishing, Farm, and Ranch Risk Management Act
provides farmers and commercial fisherman with additional capital for investment.
Document 10: Build It in America Act of 2012
make the tax credit for increasing research activities permanent.

topic.name: Health Insurance Coverage Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to health insurance coverage and services for specific medical conditions and populations.

Document 1: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA), the Public Health Service Act, and the Internal Revenue Code to require a group health plan that provides medical and surgical benefits to ensure that inpatient (and in the case of a lumpectomy, outpatient) coverage and radiation therapy are provided for breast cancer treatment.

Prohibits such a plan from restricting benefits for any hospital length of stay to less than 48 hours in connection with a mastectomy or breast conserving surgery or 24 hours in connection with a lymph node dissection.
Document 2: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to extend the temporary high risk health insurance pool program providing health insurance coverage to uninsured individuals who have a preexisting condition.
Appropriates additional funds to the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to pay claims that exceed the amount of premiums collected from the high risk pool's enrollees.
Document 3: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA), the Public Health Service Act, and the Internal Revenue Code to require a group health plan to allow a participant or beneficiary the option to seek obstetrical and gynecological physician services directly from a participating provider without a referral.

States that this Act does not prevent a plan or issuer from establishing reasonable requirements for a participating provider to communicate with the participant's or beneficiary's primary care physician.

topic.name: Land Management and Conveyance
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts related to the management, conveyance, and exchange of federal and state lands for different purposes.

Document 1: invest and manage earnings and proceeds derived from land granted for use as an agricultural college
use earnings and proceeds generated by the granted land for any uses and purposes described in that Act
Document 2: amends the Geothermal Steam Act of 1970 to allow the Department of the Interior to award noncompetitive leases on up to 640 acres of federal land for geothermal development
Interior must lease the land at fair market value
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to the city of Coffman Cove, Alaska, without consideration and by quitclaim deed, a specified parcel of National Forest System land
if any part of the land conveyed to the city ceases to be used for public purposes, the land may revert to the United States
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey specified federal land in Grand, San Juan, and Uintah Counties, Utah, to the State of Utah in exchange for specified non-federal land
provisions regarding the administration of the non-federal land after the exchange
Document 5: Establishes the Sedona-Red Rock National Scenic Area in the Coconino National Forest
requires the deposit of the proceeds received from sales or exchanges of land within the Coconino National Forest in a special fund
Document 6: Designates certain lands, in Arizona, in the Coronado National Forest as wilderness
as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System
Document 7: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, as the Rocky Mountain National Park Wilderness
providing for the inclusion of specified potential wilderness land within the Park
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, acting through the Bureau of Land Management, to convey to the city of Carlin, Nevada, all interest of the United States in such land
Requires the Secretary to determine the fair market value of the federal land based on an appraisal
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Sealaska, the regional Alaska Native Corporation for southeast Alaska, specified federal land
Subjects that conveyance to valid existing rights and the Secretary's identification and reservation
Document 10: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to convey approximately five acres of certain identified National Forest System land
Subjects the land exchange to provisions under the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976

topic.name: Fisheries and Wildlife Conservation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the conservation and management of fisheries and wildlife species, addressing overfishing, habitat restoration, and invasive species control.

Document 1: Revises and reauthorizes through FY2018 the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act (MSA).
Modifies annual catch limit requirements, including by authorizing Regional Fishery Management Councils to consider changes in an ecosystem and the economic needs of the fishing communities.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to provide assistance for projects for the conservation of rare felids and rare canids.
Establishes in the Multinational Species Conservation Fund as a separate account the Great Cats and Rare Canids Conservation Fund.
Document 3: This bill amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to apply the Act's prohibitions on importing or exporting species listed as threatened or endangered to species proposed to be listed as well.
The Government Accountability Office must conduct and submit a report on the results of a study of the effectiveness of trophy hunting in supporting international wildlife conservation efforts.
Document 5: Amends the Nonindigenous Aquatic Nuisance Prevention and Control Act of 1990 to require certain vessels operating in U.S. waters to have invasive species management plans.
Establishes the National Invasive Species Council to lead and coordinate efforts to minimize the negative effects caused by invasive species.

topic.name: STEM Education and Innovation Funding
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at promoting funding and support for STEM education and innovation through various federal programs.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2014-FY2015 to the National Science Foundation (NSF).
Requires the Director of the NSF to provide merit-reviewed, competitive grants for research on programming that engages underrepresented students in grades kindergarten through 8 in STEM.
Document 2: Directs the President to convene a National Science and Technology Summit to examine the health and direction of the United States' science, technology, engineering, and mathematics enterprises.
Requires the OSTP Director to report annually to Congress on recommendations for areas of investment for federal research and technology programs.
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act to reauthorize through FY2019 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs.
Increases by 0.1% per year, from FY2013-FY2023, the percentage of participating federal agencies' extramural research budget to be set aside for SBIR program activities.
Document 4: Requires the OSTP Director to develop and issue a set of principles to ensure the communication and open exchange of data and results to other agencies, policymakers, and the public of research conducted by a scientist employed by a federal civilian agency.

Expresses the sense of Congress that the OSTP Director should encourage all elementary and middle schools to observe a Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics Day twice in every school year.
Document 5: Increases, for both the SBIR and STTR programs, the individual small business award levels from: (1) $100,000 to $150,000, for participation at the Phase I level; and (2) $750,000 to $1 million, for participation at the Phase II level.

topic.name: Tax Policy and Reform
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments and proposals related to tax policy, focusing on credits, deductions, and alternative tax systems.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the tax credit for research expenditures
allow such credit for the basic research expenditures of educational institutions
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to deny preferential tax rates for dividends paid by a foreign corporation
revises the definition of 'qualified foreign corporation'
Document 3: Repeals the income tax, employment tax, and estate and gift tax
Imposes a national sales tax on the use or consumption in the United States
Document 4: Replaces the income tax with a flat tax of 20 percent of taxable earned income
Defines 'taxable earned income' as the excess of earned income over a standard deduction
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a 100% exclusion from gross income of the gain from the sale of qualified small business stock
Increases in 2010 and 2011 the tax deduction for business start-up expenditures
Document 6: Extends the tax deductions for state and local sales taxes
Extends through 2009 certain business-related tax provisions
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend expiring provisions relating to the alternative minimum tax
Extends through 2008 for individual taxpayers the offset of nonrefundable personal tax credits
Document 8: Provides for the treatment of qualified forest conservation bonds as tax-exempt facility bonds
Requires 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of such bonds to be used for specified forestry project costs
Document 9: Establishes new estate tax rates of between 37% and 55%
Restores the estate tax credit for any estate, inheritance, legacy, or succession taxes paid to a state
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend various provisions relating to energy production and conservation
Extends through 2009 the tax credit for producing electricity from wind facilities

topic.name: Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Reform
topic.description: Legislative amendments and acts related to the oversight, transparency, and procedures of foreign intelligence surveillance in the United States.

1: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to state that nothing in the FISA definition of 'electronic surveillance' shall be construed to encompass surveillance directed at a person located outside the United States.
Requires the AG to report semiannually to the congressional intelligence and judiciary committees on incidents of noncompliance with directives issues by the DNI and AG.
2: Prohibits a federal entity from compelling a covered person to testify or produce any document relating to protected information unless a court makes specified determinations.
Defines 'covered person' as a person engaged in journalism, including their supervisor, employer, parent, subsidiary, or affiliate.
3: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to expand government reporting requirements with respect to surveillance programs under FISA and the USA PATRIOT Act.
Permits persons receiving certain production orders to make public disclosures regarding the categories of orders to which they complied and the total number of users whose information was produced.
4: Requires the Public Printer to establish and maintain a website accessible by the public for obtaining electronic copies of all congressionally mandated reports in one place.
Prohibits GPO from charging a fee, requiring registration, or imposing any other limitation in exchange for access to the website.
5: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to include additional congressional committees to which the Attorney General is required to report under FISA.
Requires specified targeting and surveillance minimization procedures to be followed, with Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court review of such procedures.
6: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to authorize the Attorney General and Director of National Intelligence to jointly authorize targeting of persons located outside the United States.
Requires specified targeting and surveillance minimization procedures to be followed, with Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court review of such procedures.
7: Prohibits a national security letter from issuing unless a Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court finds that the information sought is relevant to an authorized investigation.
Directs the Attorney General to establish minimization and destruction procedures to ensure that information obtained is destroyed if no longer of interest.
9: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to establish a new process for the FBI to follow when applying for production of business records.
Prohibits the FBI from applying for a tangible thing production order unless a specific selection term is used as the basis for the production.
10: Amends the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004 to expand the functions of the Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board.
Directs the Attorney General to fully inform the Board about government activities under the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA).

topic.name: Education Grants and Partnerships
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts that establish grant programs and partnerships aimed at improving education, environmental literacy, and student development in schools.

Document 1: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a demonstration project awarding grants to school food authorities
Gives grant priority to school food authorities that demonstrate a severe need to improve the school environment.
Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require states to develop environmental literacy plans approved by the Secretary of Education
Directs the Secretary to award Environmental Education Professional Development Grants to states.
Document 4: Amends title II of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to include teacher and principal training in practices that address the social and emotional development needs of students
Allows funded training to include training in classroom instruction and schoolwide initiatives.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Energy to establish a research pilot program for awarding grants to certain partnerships to improve education and training in support of applied sciences in renewable energy
Enhance the quality of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) instruction at the secondary school and undergraduate levels of education.
Document 6: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to establish a geography education grant program
Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award a grant to a national nonprofit educational organization or consortium.

topic.name: Water Resource Management and Hydropower
topic.description: Legislation related to the management, delivery, and development of water resources and hydropower projects in the United States.

Document 1: facilitate hydropower development on the Diamond Fork System
prohibits any hydroelectric power generation or transmission facility on the Diamond Fork System from being financed or refinanced with any obligation
Document 2: directs the Secretary of the Interior to plan, design, and construct a water delivery pipeline
designates the Southeastern Colorado Water Conservancy District as the lead non-federal entity for the Conduit
Document 3: allows for prepayment of a specified contract between the United States and the Uintah Water Conservancy District
providing for repayment of municipal and industrial water delivery facilities
Document 5: authorizes the Cherokee Nation of Oklahoma to design and construct hydroelectric generating facilities
requires such Nation to obtain any permit required by federal or state law
Document 7: requires the Department of the Interior to implement the Agreement between the United States and Westlands Water District
the bill amends the 1960 statute that authorized Interior to construct the San Luis Unit
Document 8: authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to convey all U.S. rights to the Goleta Water Distribution System
shields the United States from liability for damages relating to the conveyed property
Document 9: authorizes the Bureau of Reclamation to participate in projects to benefit eligible communities
capture and treat wastewater and to distribute recycled water
Document 10: requires the Blackfeet Tribe to enter into an agreement for the exercise of the respective water rights
authorizes the Tribe to develop and market hydroelectric power from the Unit

topic.name: Veterans Benefits and Support
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing benefits and support for veterans and their families, including disability compensation, burial benefits, and education assistance.

Document 1: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act to provide for full benefits for disabled widows and widowers without regard to age or any previous reduction in OASDI benefits.
Declares that there shall be no reduction in the widow's or widower's insurance benefit of an individual for any month in which such individual is under a disability.
Document 2: Allows an individual to elect to receive retired pay for non-regular (reserve) service upon retirement for service performed in an active reserve status.

Requires the recomputation of reserve retired pay and, if appropriate, the adjustment of the retired grade of reserve retirees to reflect the successful completion of at least two years of post-retirement service in an active reserve status.
Document 3: This bill requires the Federal Retirement Thrift Investment Board to prescribe regulations to permit continued participation in the Thrift Savings Plan (TSP) by a member of the uniformed services after such member's discharge or release from service if such individual is entitled to veterans disability compensation.
Such individual's monthly veterans disability compensation shall be deemed to be his or her basic pay for a monthly pay period.

topic.name: Employee Retirement and Pension Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on employee retirement plans, pension transparency, and tax implications for retirement benefits.

1: excludes from the gross income of an employee shares of employer securities received in a qualified employee stock distribution
2: deny tax benefits relating to bonds issued by a state or political subdivision during any period in which such state or political subdivision is noncompliant with specified reporting requirements
3: allows commercial airline employees to transfer to a traditional individual retirement account (IRA)
excludes from the gross income of such employees any such amount received from an airline
4: require certain employers who do not maintain qualifying retirement plans to make available to their eligible employees a payroll deposit individual retirement account
5: requires plan sponsors of a state or local government employee pension benefit plan to file with the Secretary of the Treasury a report for each plan year
6: amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA) and the Internal Revenue Code with respect to the formula for the target normal plan cost element
7: American Pension Investments Modernization Act of 2014
requires the fiduciary of an employee benefit plan to establish policies for appointment of an investment manager
8: Coal Accountability and Retired Employee Act of 2010
requires the transfer during any fiscal year to the trustees of the 1974 United Mine Workers of America (UMWA) Pension Plan
9: Pension Protection Technical Corrections Act of 2007
modifies rules governing defined benefit and defined contribution pension plans
10: Sensible Transparency for Retirement Plans Act of 2009
requires a service provider to disclose to the plan administrator the services to be provided and the total compensation to be received for them

topic.name: Designation of Federal Buildings
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative measures related to the naming and designation of various federal buildings and post office facilities across the United States.

Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 3624 Commercial Street Southeast in Salem, Oregon, as the "Sue Miller Post Office Building."

Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 4320 Blue Parkway in Kansas City, Missouri, as the "Wallace S. Hartsfield Post Office Building."
Document 7: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1900 West Gray Street in Houston, Texas, as the "Hazel Hainsworth Young Post Office Building."
Document 8: Designates the U.S. Federal Judicial Center and the U.S. courthouse located at 333 West Broadway in San Diego, California, as the "John Rhoades Federal Judicial Center" and the "James M. Carter and Judith N. Keep United States Courthouse," respectively.

Document 10: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 2024 Jerome Avenue, in Bronx, New York, as the "Dr. Roscoe C. Brown, Jr. Post Office Building."

topic.name: Trade Adjustment Assistance
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures related to Trade Adjustment Assistance programs aimed at supporting workers and communities affected by trade policies.

Document 1: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to extend duty-free treatment under the Generalized System of Preferences (GSP)
Amends the Trade and Globalization Adjustment Assistance Act of 2009 to repeal the December 31, 2011, termination date for trade adjustment assistance (TAA) programs.
Document 2: Amends the Trade and Globalization Adjustment Assistance Act of 2009 to extend trade adjustment assistance (TAA) programs through February 12, 2011.
Requires funding levels for TAA programs under prior law to apply beginning February 13, 2011.

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Rights
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to abortion, parental consent, and informed consent requirements, reflecting the legal landscape surrounding reproductive rights in the United States.

Document 1: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
deems such transporting to be a de facto abridgment of the right of a parent
Document 2: require abortion providers to perform an obstetric ultrasound
provide a simultaneous explanation of what the ultrasound is depicting
Document 4: prohibit any person from performing or attempting to perform an abortion except in conformity with this Act's requirements
prohibits the abortion from being performed if the probable post-fertilization age of the unborn child is 20 weeks or greater
Document 5: imposes criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly or knowingly attempts to perform an abortion based on the sex or gender of the child
defines 'sex-selection abortion' as an abortion undertaken to eliminate an unborn child based on the sex or gender of the child
Document 7: declares that it is the policy of the United States that every woman has the fundamental right to choose to terminate a pregnancy prior to fetal viability
prohibits a federal, state, or local governmental entity from denying or interfering with a woman's right to exercise such choices

topic.name: Regulatory and Legislative Actions
topic.description: This cluster discusses various regulatory and legislative measures related to immigration, environmental protection, consumer rights, and state versus federal law issues.

Document 2: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from prohibiting, limiting, or controlling any type of firearm ammunition or fishing tackle based on material composition.
Document 3: Vet Electronic Trails Act of 2015

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing the welfare, protection, and rights of children, including those at risk of abuse, neglect, or exploitation.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to take specified actions to allow the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to better serve persons with limited English proficiency by providing technical assistance to recipients of federal funds.

Document 3: Directs the Assistant Secretary for Children and Families of the Department of Health and Human Services to require each location of a covered program to meet specified minimum standards if individually or together with other locations it has an effect on interstate commerce.
establish a process for conducting unannounced site inspections of each covered program location to determine compliance with such standards.
Document 4: Amends the Missing Children's Assistance Act to require the Office of Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention's annual grant to the National Center for Missing and Exploited Children to be used to operate a cyber tipline.
reporting on missing children to provide, with respect to each missing child report, a recent photograph of the child, if available.
Document 5: Amends the Public Health Service Act to reauthorize and revise a program to award grants to strengthen the response of state, tribal, territorial, or local health care systems to domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, and stalking.
provide training to health care professionals on domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault and stalking.
Document 6: Directs the Attorney General to establish a national Silver Alert communications network within the Department of Justice (DOJ) to assist regional and local search efforts for missing seniors.
establish minimum standards for the issuance of alerts through the network.
Document 7: Amends the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 to require a national criminal background check for an individual who is a child care staff member.
Makes a child care provider ineligible for Program funds if the criminal background check reveals an adult felony conviction.
Document 8: Authorizes the Attorney General to maintain public databases, known as the National Missing and Unidentified Persons System or NamUs, to contain missing persons records and unidentified remains cases.
share information on missing persons and unidentified human remains contained in the National Crime Information Center (NCIC) database with the NamUs databases.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a pilot program to make grants to public and nonprofit entities to provide substance abuse treatment services in the form of long-term counseling.
services that facilitate interaction between individuals receiving treatment for substance abuse and individuals receiving prevention services.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to develop guidelines for use by state child welfare agencies in training appropriate child welfare employees and court employees in identifying, documenting, educating, and counseling children at risk of becoming victims of trafficking.
requires the Secretary to develop guidelines that contain recommendations on how state child welfare agencies may prevent children from becoming victims of trafficking.

Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of Health and Human Services to provide certain services for birth parents who have placed a child for adoption
Specifies services for birth parents including counseling and training of mental health professionals.
