Document 6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to extend the annual cap exemption for returning H-2B aliens.

topic.name: Consumer Safety and Health Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at ensuring the safety and health of consumers through regulations related to food, products, and environmental safety.

Document 1: Child Nicotine Poisoning Prevention Act of 2014 - Directs the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to promulgate a rule requiring liquid nicotine containers to be designed with special packaging that is difficult for children under five years of age to open or to obtain harmful contents from.
Document 2: Safe Agriculture Production Act of 2015 - This bill amends the Plant Protection Act to allow state, local, or tribal governments to use methyl bromide as a fumigant if...
Document 3: Consumer Recall Protection Act of 2011 - Prohibits a person from selling to consumers any covered product that is subject to a recall.
Document 4: Human and Pet Food Safety Act of 2007 - Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to require a person that has reason to believe that any food introduced into interstate commerce may be in violation of the FFDCA to immediately notify the Secretary of the Health and Human Services.
Document 5: Advancing Standards in Regenerative Medicine Act - This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to facilitate the establishment of a public-private Standards Coordinating Body in Regenerative Medicine and Advanced Therapies.
Document 6: Commercial Seafood Consumer Protection Act - Directs the Secretary of Commerce (Secretary) to establish a program to strengthen federal activities for ensuring that commercially distributed seafood meets federal food quality and safety requirements.
Document 7: Lead Poisoning Reduction Act of 2007 - Amends the Toxic Substances Control Act to establish a Select Group on Lead Exposure.
Document 8: Ban Poisonous Additives Act of 2014 - Prohibits the distribution of a food if its container is composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A (BPA) or can release BPA into food.
Document 9: Food Labeling Modernization Act of 2015 - This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to require the front of food packages to bear a nutrition label that complies with Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regulations.
Document 10: Cleaning Product Right to Know Act of 2016 - This bill requires cleaning products to bear a label with, and requires manufacturers to include on their websites, a list of all of the product's ingredients.

topic.name: Wildlife and Environmental Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating the protection and management of wildlife and natural resources in the United States.

Document 1: Sets forth procedures for admission into the United States of the state of New Columbia.
Provides that New Columbia shall consist of all territory of the District as of the enactment of this Act.
Document 2: Prohibits any person from importing, exporting, transporting, selling, receiving, acquiring, purchasing, breeding, possessing, or owning any prohibited wildlife species.
Defines 'breeding' as facilitating the reproduction of prohibited wildlife species.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to prescribe regulatory requirements for the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to license, train, assign, and oversee persons who are to be hired by the management of horse shows.
Prohibits a person from causing or directing a horse to become sore for the purpose of any horse show.
Document 4: Requires that there be an independent anti-doping organization with responsibility for ensuring the integrity and safety of horse races.
Developing, publishing, and maintaining rules regarding substances, methods, and treatments that may and may not be administered to a horse.
Document 5: Prohibit the sale or transfer of any free-roaming horse or burro or its remains on U.S. public lands for consideration for processing into commercial products.
Document 6: Prohibits the Secretaries of Agriculture and the Interior from promulgating or enforcing any regulation that prohibit an individual from possessing firearms in any unit administered by the U.S. Forest Service.
Document 7: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 by requiring the Department of Interior and the Department of Commerce to obtain the consent of each state for adding species to the list of threatened or endangered plants and animals.
Interior must submit to Congress a list of all endangered or threatened species.
Document 8: Severs the United States relations with the Cherokee Nation of Oklahoma until the Cherokee Nation is meeting all of its treaty obligations.
Requires a report from the Government Accountability Office (GAO) on the Cherokee Nation's expenditure of federal funds.
Document 9: Directs the Office of Justice Programs of the Department of Justice (DOJ) to carry out, with up to nine Indian tribes in Alaska, the Alaska Safe Families and Villages Demonstration Project.
Permits a participating tribe to impose specified sanctions, including incarceration.
Document 10: Prohibits any person from offering billfish or billfish products for sale, selling them, or having custody, control, or possession of them for purposes of offering them for sale or selling them.

topic.name: Educational Funding and Standards
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative acts aimed at improving funding, standards, and accountability in elementary and secondary education.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to allocate funds to states for competitive matching grants to local educational agencies (LEAs) for school repair, renovation, and construction.
Requires the National Center for Education Statistics to study the condition of public elementary and secondary schools.
Document 2: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require states and local educational agencies that receive school improvement funds under such part to ensure that their schools provide: (1) 150 minutes of physical education per week for elementary school students.
225 minutes of physical education per week for middle and secondary school students.
Document 3: Amends the National Assessment of Educational Progress Authorization Act to require the Commissioner for Educational Statistics to add science to the mandatory biennial national and state academic achievement assessments of students in grades four, eight, and twelve.
Requires states receiving school improvement funds to participate in such science assessments.
Document 4: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to allow states to include measures of individual or cohort growth over time in determining whether students are making adequate yearly progress toward state academic performance standards.
Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to state educational agencies to develop or increase the capacity of data systems for accountability purposes.
Document 5: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to allow local educational agencies (LEAs) that are unable to provide a small, rural, or remote school with a highly-qualified on-site classroom teacher for a core academic subject to use distance education.
Gives schools which are identified as needing improvement an extra year to make AYP before being required to allow student transfers.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Education to make matching grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17, to implement state middle school needs analyses and improvement plans.
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a majority of middle grade students matriculate to high schools with graduation rates below 60%.
Document 7: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to allocate funds to states for competitive school repair and renovation grants to local educational agencies (LEAs).
Requires each state to reserve a portion of their allocation for a state-level database of public school facility inventory, condition, design, and utilization.
Document 8: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to alter the requirement that teachers be highly qualified by applying it only to teachers of core subjects.
Provides additional funding for rural teacher recruitment, retention, and professional development activities.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of Education to award renewable two-year grants to states to establish or improve a Seal of Biliteracy program to recognize student proficiency in speaking, reading, and writing in both English and a second language.
Requires states to provide participating students who demonstrate that proficiency a permanent seal or other marker on their secondary school diploma.
Document 10: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to allow states, when determining whether disabled or limited English proficient student subgroups are making adequate yearly progress, to include in such subgroups students who are no longer identified as disabled or limited English proficient.
Requires states to consider the percentage of poor children LEAs serve in their AYP assessments.

topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of U.S. foreign military policy and funding related to military operations, security measures, and international relations, particularly in Iraq and the Middle East.

Requires the withdrawal of all units and members of U.S. Armed Forces deployed in Iraq.
Document 2: Establishes reporting requirements for military operations in Iraq
Expresses the sense of Congress regarding contractor fraud or war profiteering.
Document 5: Prohibits the establishment of a permanent U.S. military presence in Iraq
Directs the President to report to Congress outlining a strategy to end U.S. combat operations in Iraq.
Document 4: Authorizes appropriations for foreign language training for Department of State employees
Expresses the sense of Congress concerning priorities of the program and funding through interagency agreements.

topic.name: Charter School Programs and Education Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing charter school programs and educational leadership through grants and technical assistance.

improve the efficiency or throughput, the safety, and the environmental sustainability of a national or regional transportation network.

Document 3: amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to require the Director for Domestic Nuclear Detection to establish the Securing the Cities program to enhance the ability of the United States to detect and prevent terrorist attacks.

assist state, local, tribal, and territorial governments in designing and implementing, or enhancing existing, architectures for coordinated and integrated detection and interdiction of nuclear or other radiological materials.
Document 4: Establishes within the Department of the Interior the Rare Earth Policy Task Force to assist federal agencies in reviewing federal, state, local, and tribal laws, regulations, and policies that discourage investment, exploration, and development of domestic rare earths.

submit biennially to certain congressional committees a plan for research, development, demonstration, and commercial application to ensure the long-term, secure, and sustainable supply of rare earth materials.
Document 5: Directs the President to establish a primary mechanism to assist the private sector in coordinating U.S. development programs with private sector investment activities.
include private sector consultation in all country, sector, and global development strategies.
Document 6: implementation of the United States National Action Plan on Women, Peace, and Security (NAP) is paramount in improving the lives of women around the world.
ensure that the tenets of NAP are incorporated into programs for conflict prevention, humanitarian and disaster response, peacekeeping, and democracy promotion.
Document 7: Establishes a congressional Commission on the Abolition of Modern-day Slavery which shall study matters relating to modern-day slavery.
evaluate comparative strategies to prevent modern-day slavery, rescue and rehabilitate its victims, and prosecute traffickers.
Document 8: Directs the President to create a National Intermodal Transportation Planning Task Force to study the nation's transportation needs and priorities for the next 30 years.
draft a National Intermodal Transportation plan that includes short- and long-term goals for meeting those priorities.
Document 9: Directs the Administrator of FEMA to establish a program to make grants to state and local governments to assist them in projects that improve the ability of trauma center hospitals and/or airports in hazardous seismic zones to withstand an earthquake.
require each state and local government, as a condition for receiving a grant, to submit a comprehensive strategic earthquake response plan.
Document 10: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to create a separate website about the reports issued by each Office of Inspector General.
include a general description of the role of the Office of Inspector General.

topic.name: Medicare Coverage Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at expanding and improving Medicare coverage for specific medical services and treatments.

Document 2: Medicare Home Infusion Therapy Coverage Act of 2007 - Provides for Medicare coverage of home infusion therapy and home infusion drugs.

Document 3: Drug Rebate Equalization Act of 2009 - Amends title XIX (Medicaid) to reduce prescription drug costs for Medicaid managed care.
Extends discounts offered under fee-for-service Medicaid plans.
Document 4: CT Colonography Screening for Colorectal Cancer Act of 2012 - Provides Medicare coverage for screening computed tomography colonography.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to report on the status of coverage.
Document 5: Preserving Access to Modern Prosthetic Limbs Act of 2016 - Postpones implementation of a local coverage determination on lower limb prostheses.
Provides guidance related to LCDs by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid.

CMS shall help educate beneficiaries on the program availability.

Document 5: Establishes an independent Financial Stability Council to review, and approve or prohibit issuance of, any rule or regulation issued by any federal financial regulator
Grants Council actions precedence in any conflict with any regulatory action of a federal financial regulator
Document 6: Amends the Community Reinvestment Act of 1977 with respect to the requirement that, in examining a financial institution, the appropriate federal financial supervisory agency assess and take into account the institution's record of meeting the credit needs of its entire community
Requires the appropriate federal financial supervisory agency to prescribe regulations that reward a regulated financial institution that receives a high rating
Document 7: Amends the Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council Act of 1978 to require a federal financial institutions regulatory agency to make a final examination report to a financial institution within 60 days
Grants a financial institution the right to appeal a material supervisory determination contained in a final report of examination
Document 8: Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to publish in the Federal Register proposed revisions to the Small Bank Holding Company Policy Statement
Authorizes the Board, however, to exclude any bank holding company or savings and loan holding company from the policy statement if that is warranted for supervisory purposes
Document 9: Requires the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, and other agencies to consider the risk profile and business models of individual financial institutions
Each agency shall conduct a review of its regulatory actions
Document 10: Establishes the Financial Services Systemic Risk Oversight Council to regulate systemic risk in the U.S. financial system
Requires the Council to monitor financial markets and financial companies to identify and address potential systemic risk

topic.name: Medicare and Social Security Amendments
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various amendments to the Social Security Act related to Medicare, Medicaid, and identity protection for beneficiaries.

Document 1: Amends part B (Supplementary Medical Insurance) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide for coverage of the shingles vaccine and its administration.
Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act with respect to prescription drug plans.
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act with respect to prescription drug plans to repeal the prohibition against: (1) interference by the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) with negotiations.
Grants the Secretary authority similar to that of other federal entities that purchase prescription drugs in bulk to negotiate contracts with manufacturers of covered part D drugs.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to create an exception to Medicare secondary payer requirements for certain workers' compensation settlement agreements.
Provides for the satisfaction of such requirements through use of qualified Medicare set-asides under such agreements.
Document 4: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to revise the average sales price methodology for payment for drugs and biologicals under Medicare part B.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to increase the payment amount for a drug or biological to ensure that it is in no case less than the widely available market price.
Document 5: Amends part A (General Provisions) of title XI of the Social Security Act to make Guam and the Virgin Islands eligible for the Supplemental Security Income program.
Eligibility for the Supplemental Security Income program for the Aged, Blind, and Disabled under SSA title XVI.
Document 6: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act to prohibit the use of Social Security account numbers on identification cards.
Declares the sense of Congress that private entities should not use Social Security account numbers as a method identifying individuals.
Document 7: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance) (OASDI) of the Social Security Act to prohibit the use of Social Security account numbers on identification cards.
Declares the sense of Congress that private entities should not use Social Security account numbers as a method identifying individuals.
Document 9: Amends part B (Supplemental Medical Insurance) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act with respect to adjustments to Medicare parts B and D premiums for high income beneficiaries.
Requires reduction of the monthly amount of the Medicare parts B and D premium subsidies for individuals whose modified adjusted gross income exceeds the threshold amount.
Document 10: Amends titles XI and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to remove the cap on Medicaid payments for Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, and American Samoa.
